user_request_summary: Enforce Master Spec Rule #7 — closed payroll periods cannot be modified. Add domain EnsureOpen() method, PayrollPeriodClosedException, 7 write guards across repositories/services, unit tests for guards, and integration tests for HTTP 409.

included.rationale: Core work: domain method, exception class, 7 guard insertions, unit tests, integration tests
Verify guard completeness, test coverage, exception handling consistency, no regressions

excluded.rationale: Simple domain pattern (EnsureOpen guard + custom exception). No new APIs, no data model changes, no ADRs needed. Pattern already established by VoucherNotFoundException.
No infrastructure changes. No new dependencies, Dockerfile changes, CI pipeline steps, or deployment configs.
Internal domain enforcement rule. No new user-facing features, API endpoints, or UI screens.

splitting_decision.keep_rationale: Single-layer cohesion — all changes are in NursingCareBackend (Domain, Infrastructure, Application, Tests). Single test harness (xUnit). Estimated under 4 hours. No cross-repo or cross-deployment-artifact concerns.

what_exists: PayrollPeriod entity with Status property (Open/Closed enum)
PayrollRecalculationService filters Open periods but does not throw on Closed
VoucherNotFoundException establishes exception pattern in Application/Exceptions
what_needs_building: Unit tests for EnsureOpen() and each guard
Integration tests verifying HTTP 409 Conflict on closed-period write attempts

infrastructure_status.seed_data: SYNCED — Closed period exists with 10 payroll lines

resolved_from_config.question: Test framework?
resolved_from_config.answer: xUnit + WebApplicationFactory with real SQL Server

resolved_from_config.question: Infrastructure available?
resolved_from_config.answer: Docker + SQL Server running, seed data synced with Closed period

resolved_from_config.question: Where to place PayrollPeriodClosedException?
resolved_from_config.answer: Domain/Payroll/ — it is a domain invariant violation, not an application-layer exception

resolved_from_config.question: HTTP status code for closed-period write attempt?
resolved_from_config.answer: 409 Conflict — represents a state conflict (period is closed)
